sweeps.summary: Bootstrap real-data sweep on 2026-05-06. Searched DLD/RERA, developer launches, auctions, market-data and brokerage/luxury sources via WebSearch + WebFetch. The freshest tier-1 verifiable Dubai-property news was the DLD Q1 2026 print (April 9), the AGBI/Khaleej Times/National coverage of the AED 750K visa-floor removal (April 29–May 1) and a Voice of Emirates weekly transactions piece (May 2) — all outside the 72h date cap. Per the empty-sweep-is-success rule, no items added; sources logged.

Real-data bootstrap sweep on 2026-05-06 with one-shot 216h date-cap waiver (see SWEEP_MEMORY 2026-05-06-A). Three verified items added: the AED 750K visa-floor removal (seismic), the Khaleej Times $10M+ deals print (major) and the weekly DLD transactions piece (notable). All URLs and og:images fetched and confirmed 200. Eight platform-seed cards left in place — user may decide separately whether to remove.

added.title: Dubai removes AED 750K floor on 2-year property-investor visa; sub-AED 750K stock now visa-eligible
added.note: Added under visa.

added.title: Dubai $10M+ deals jump 63% Y/Y in Q1 2026; residential sales cross AED 180B with AED 422M Aman Residences leading
added.note: Added under luxury.

added.title: Dubai weekly real-estate print: AED 15.2B across 3,866 transactions; Business Bay leads sales at AED 767M
added.note: Added under market.

sweeps.summary: Two May 4–5 Khaleej Times prints frame a market that is moderating, not crashing: April closed at AED 48B/13,977 deals while ValuStrat logged its first quarterly index contraction since 2020, and brokers report resale clearing within 5–15% of peak rather than at distress.

added.title: Dubai April 2026 sales hit AED 48B across 13,977 deals; ValuStrat flags first quarterly index contraction since 2020
added.note: Major monthly DLD print: AED 48B / 13,977 deals plus ValuStrat's first negative quarter since 2020 — reframes the cooling cycle for both buyers and sellers.

added.title: Dubai resale clears within 5–15% of peak, not distress: brokers see negotiation, not panic, in Marina, Downtown, Business Bay
added.note: Three-broker survey (haus & haus, White & Co, Bayut) defines the operating range — resale within 5–15% of peak, no distress — useful market color for any sourcing or negotiation playbook.

sweeps.summary: Two new tier-1 Dubai signals dated 2026-05-06: Binghatti's first villa-community pre-launch in Al Rowaiyah/Dubailand drew 10,000+ visitors, and Gulf News broke a record AED 400M Jumeirah Coastline land assembly setting a fresh super-prime comp.

added.title: Binghatti pre-launches Tilal Binghatti, its first villa community in Dubai (Al Rowaiyah, Dubailand) — 10,000+ visitors at EOI event
added.note: Added under developer.

added.title: Dubai super-prime land print: 3 Jumeirah Coastline plots assemble for AED 400M (113,000+ sqft, 160m beachfront) with AED 1B+ projected GDV
added.note: Added under luxury.

sweeps.summary: Verified one new opportunity within the 72h cap: betterhomes/H&H launched City Tower, a 93-storey, 695-residence long-term leasing tower on Sheikh Zayed Road, with move-ins from June 2026. No qualifying same-window auction, distress, DLD-print, or developer-launch items beyond what the feed already covers.

added.title: betterhomes + H&H launch City Tower: 93-storey, 695-residence long-term leasing block on Sheikh Zayed Road, move-ins from June 2026
added.note: Added under rental.

sweeps.summary: Two fresh Dubai-property signals from the early-week DLD data: (a) an ultra-prime AED 101.2M Casa AHS Sky Villa transaction on the Dubai Water Canal, and (b) a 5-May DLD daily print where Lumena by Omniyat alone contributed AED 1.02B of off-plan office volume. No portal-level distress or duplicate signals strong enough to ship after dedupe.

added.title: Ultra-prime Dubai print: AED 101.2M Casa AHS Sky Villa sale on Dubai Water Canal, AHS Properties' Fendi Casa-finished tower
added.note: Added under luxury.

added.title: Dubai DLD daily print 2026-05-05: AED 2.39B with off-plan at 78% — Lumena by Omniyat alone contributes AED 1.02B in single-day commercial sales
added.note: Added under commercial.

sweeps.summary: 06-May-2026 DLD daily print follows the AED 2.39B 05-May day with AED 1.47B; Business Bay offices stay the headline trade as 32 secondary-market deals clear AED 646M, with Lumena alone resyncing AED 558.5M across 20 transactions and HQ by Rove + Lumena Alta filling the rest.

added.title: Dubai DLD daily print 2026-05-06: AED 1.47B with Business Bay offices clearing AED 646M — Lumena books another AED 558.5M day across 20 deals
added.note: Fresh DLD daily print for 06-May-2026 (published 07-May): AED 1.47B day with Business Bay offices taking AED 646M on the back of 20 Lumena resales. Distinct from the existing 05-May print card and supported by independent Gulf Today reporting on the office burst.

sweeps.summary: 2026-05-07T07Z sweep: two fresh Khaleej Times market-thesis cards added — long-term doubling-money narrative with off-plan +3.3% / ready -16% YTD divergence, plus Savills survey showing 45% of UAE buyers planning to transact in 12 months and a clear pivot to ready stock (60%) over off-plan (23%). No fresh distress, auction or duplicate-unit signals cleared the bar; portal pages remain JS-rendered and produced no new card-grade leads.

added.title: Khaleej Times: Dubai owners still doubling money post-softening — off-plan +3.3% YoY in April but ready market down 16% as ValuStrat flags first contraction since 2020
added.note: Major-importance KT thesis piece (07-May-2026) with concrete YTD data — distinct from existing 'resale within 5–15% of peak' card because the new angle is the off-plan +3.3% vs ready -16% divergence and the long-hold doubling-money thesis.

added.title: Savills UAE buyer-intent survey: 45% plan to buy within 12 months, 60% prefer ready over off-plan (23%), only 4% of owners considering a sale
added.note: Notable-importance Savills survey (06-May-2026) introducing the first quantified buyer-intent data point of the cycle — material because the 60% ready preference inverts the transaction-side off-plan dominance and the 4% sell-rate quantifies the no-distress claim.

sweeps.summary: Scheduled sweep on 2026-05-07. Added one verified developer launch — Palmridge's Villea West in Jumeirah Garden City — picked up via the May 6/7 wire round. Other surfaced angles (Cavendish Maxwell 4-month residential print, April off-plan office record, City Tower update) overlapped existing feed cards or fell outside the 72h window.

added.title: Palmridge launches Villea West in Jumeirah Garden City: 229 fully-furnished studios + 1-beds, Woods Bagot design, Q4 2027 handover
added.note: Added under developer.

sweeps.summary: Cron sweep on 2026-05-07: scanned the every-sweep portal, official-data, developer, brokerage and market-data sources plus secondary tier-1 newsrooms. Fresh signals in the 72h window were either already in feed (DLD daily prints, visa-floor change, ValuStrat softening, Palmridge Villea West) or unverifiable (Brookfield/Alshaya 480k sqft Dubai Hills JV is behind a Bloomberg paywall and not yet on the official Brookfield UAE page). Jamal Living's 2026-05-06 Al Barsha launch announcement carries no project name, unit count, price or handover, so it fails the concrete-metrics inclusion bar. No new cards.

May 7 2026 sweep: Brookfield + Alshaya formed a Dubai Hills mixed-use JV — Brookfield's first big-ticket Dubai commitment since the regional conflict and a fresh institutional-capital read for office/residential underwriting in the master community.

added.title: Brookfield + Alshaya form Dubai Hills mixed-use JV: 480,000 sqft of offices, residences and retail in the Emaar/Meraas master community
added.note: Fresh same-day institutional-capital signal: 480k sqft Brookfield+Alshaya JV in Dubai Hills, verified across AGBI (full piece), Bloomberg (headline + lede) and MEED (paywalled but corroborates date, partners, size). Inclusion bar = official market signal that changes pricing/supply read for Dubai Hills and surrounding office/retail comps.

sweeps.summary: 2026-05-07T15:00Z sweep — added two policy/infrastructure signals dated 2026-05-07: the UAE Golden Visa bank-guarantee rule that supersedes the 50% cash-upfront requirement (different visa class to the existing AED 750K floor item — this targets the AED 2M/10-year Golden Visa for mortgage and off-plan buyers) and the 85% completion milestone on the AED 431M Dubai Harbour bridge to Sheikh Zayed Road (Aug-2026 opening, 24 residential towers benefit). Cross-checked the Brookfield/Alshaya, Tilal Binghatti, ALA Developments, Cavendish Maxwell 4-month and AED 19.7B off-plan apartment stories — all already in feed or duplicate. Off-plan office record story (AED 3B April / AED 9.4B YTD) collides with existing daily-print URL; folded as supporting context. Symbolic Zen Residences Jetour T2 promo skipped (no disclosed unit prices, fails diligence bar).

added.title: Dubai Golden Visa: bank guarantee replaces 50% cash upfront for AED 2M property route — mortgage and off-plan buyers can now apply at contract signing
added.note: PropertyNews.ae confirmation 2026-05-07 of GDRFA bank-guarantee rule that supersedes the 50% cash-upfront test for AED 2M Golden Visa property route — distinct from the AED 750K 2-year residency visa change already in feed; together they materially widen the property-visa funnel.

added.title: Dubai Harbour Bridge hits 85% completion: 1.5 km link to Sheikh Zayed Road opens August 2026, cuts 24-tower commute from 20 min to 3 min
added.note: PropertyNews.ae confirmation 2026-05-07 that the AED 431M RTA/Shamal Holding bridge to Dubai Harbour is 85% built with August 2026 opening — concrete commute-time delta (~20 min → ~3 min) drives a quantifiable comp re-rate for 24 residential towers in the cluster.

sweeps.summary: Scheduled sweep on 2026-05-07: surveyed portal feeds, DLD daily prints, developer announcements and brokerage releases. One genuinely new item makes the bar — Symbolic Developments' Jetour T2 SUV bundle for Zen Residences buyers (May launch promo). Most fresh news threads (Bloomberg/AGBI Brookfield, KT Savills survey, May 6 DLD daily print, Jumeirah Coastline land deal, AED 48B April print) are already represented in the existing 17-item feed and were skipped to avoid duplicates.

added.title: Symbolic Developments bundles a Jetour T2 SUV with every Zen Residences home in May — AED 210M Al Furjan launch with 50/50 plan and Q2 2027 handover
added.note: Boutique Al Furjan launch with a calendar-month SUV incentive — a fresh, dated, verifiable developer signal that does not duplicate any of the 17 items already in the feed.
